error.signature: ISO 8601 duration P1M parsed as 1 minute instead of 1 month, or vice versa

verdict.summary: ISO 8601 durations use M for both months (P1M) and minutes (PT1M). P1M = 1 month, PT1M = 1 minute. The T separator is critical. Many parsers and developers confuse P1M30S (1 month 30 seconds) with PT1M30S (1 minute 30 seconds).

dead_ends.action: Use P1M30S to mean 1 minute and 30 seconds
dead_ends.why_fails: P1M30S means 1 month and 30 seconds, NOT 1 minute 30 seconds. The T separator is required before time components: PT1M30S.

dead_ends.action: Parse ISO 8601 durations with simple regex
dead_ends.why_fails: Duration semantics are complex: P1M = 28-31 days depending on month. P1Y = 365 or 366 days. Simple regex can't capture calendar-dependent semantics.

workarounds.action: Always include T separator before time components in durations
workarounds.how: PT1H30M = 1 hour 30 minutes; P1DT12H = 1 day 12 hours; P1M = 1 month (not 1 minute!)

workarounds.action: Use isodate or dateutil library for robust ISO 8601 duration parsing
Use total seconds representation for unambiguous duration serialization
workarounds.how: Store durations as seconds (integer) internally. Convert to ISO 8601 only for API responses.
